metaTitle: Image Compressor — hugdot (Free, Private & No Upload)
metaDescription: Securely compress up to 20 images at once with hugdot. 100% private, local browser processing. Your photos never leave your device. Fast, simple, and safe.
introSummary: Welcome to hugdot, where your digital privacy is our priority. Our Batch Image Compressor is designed to handle up to 20 images simultaneously without ever sending a single byte to a server. By processing everything locally on your device, hugdot ensures your sensitive photos, IDs, and creative works remain 100% in your control. Experience lightning-fast, secure optimization that 'hugs' your data and protects your privacy.

contextUsage.title: For Privacy-Conscious Creators
contextUsage.desc: Protect your original work. hugdot removes hidden metadata (EXIF) and GPS tags during batch compression, ensuring your privacy before you share online.

contextUsage.title: For Secure Business Docs
contextUsage.desc: Need to compress a batch of sensitive IDs or legal scans? hugdot's zero-upload architecture is the safest way to handle confidential documents.

contextUsage.title: For Efficient Web Workflow
contextUsage.desc: Optimize entire folders of website assets in seconds. Convert them to WebP and download as a single ZIP—all while staying offline.

howto.description: Drag up to 20 files into our secure zone. They load instantly into your browser's memory, not our server.
Adjust the target slider. hugdot's engine will calculate the best balance between small file size and crisp visual quality.
Our parallel processing engine handles your batch in real-time. You'll see exactly how much space you're saving per image.
Review the preview and click 'Save All'. hugdot bundles your optimized files into a neat ZIP archive for instant use.

why: The hugdot Privacy Guarantee — We physically cannot see your images. Everything happens 100% on your local machine.
Smart Batch Processing — High-speed parallel optimization for up to 20 images, saving you hours of manual work.
Memory-Safe Engine — Engineered to prevent browser crashes, even on mobile devices, using dynamic URL revocation.
WebP Native Support — Industry-leading compression ratios by converting bulky PNGs/JPGs into modern WebP format.
No Strings Attached — No registration, no ads, and no daily limits. Just a clean, safe tool for your daily needs.
Eco-Friendly Performance — Local processing uses less energy than uploading/downloading large files to a data center.

faq.q: Is hugdot really safer than other sites?
faq.a: Yes. Most sites 'take' your image to their server. hugdot 'hugs' it locally—it stays inside your browser tab and disappears the moment you close it.

faq.q: What is the maximum file size?
faq.a: Since it uses your device's RAM, there's no fixed limit, but we recommend images under 50MB for the smoothest experience on mobile.
Zero. We don't even have a database for your images. hugdot is a pure client-side utility tool.
Absolutely! Once the page is loaded, you can turn off your internet and hugdot will still compress your images perfectly.

technicalSpecs.useCase: Server-grade compression logic running in your browser
Optimized for speed and device stability
Trusted for medical, legal, and private data
Instant organization for multiple optimized files

troubleshooting: Image size didn't change? If an image is already highly compressed, hugdot won't force it smaller to avoid quality ruin. Try 'WebP' mode.
Processing stopped? hugdot needs your tab to be active for some browsers to give it full CPU power. Keep the window open until it hits 100%.
Missing files in ZIP? Ensure you have enough disk space. hugdot generates the ZIP in memory before saving it to your downloads folder.
proTips: Use hugdot as a privacy filter. Even if you don't need much compression, running photos through hugdot strips hidden location data (GPS) automatically.
For lightning-fast results on a laptop, plug in your power cable. hugdot can then use your CPU's 'Turbo' mode for the batch.
Want the best SEO? Aim for the 70-80% quality range on hugdot—it's the sweet spot for Google's PageSpeed Insights.
